finding_results.notes: Aldric now distinguishes observation from inference before and after the reveal. The campsite scene includes an immediate bowl test: the hum narrows into the ceramic, ash climbs and gathers along the bowl, and the quest-light briefly constricts. Maren's Crook-first decision now follows from watching the bowl do real work while Aldric and Vess admit the larger theory is still partly inferential.
The bowl description keeps the chipped-rim/market-glaze identifier but cuts the repeated eleven-year prop baseline and replaces it with a forward-looking physical mismatch against the new hum.
The chapter was expanded with causally necessary material in the bowl-test sequence and aftermath while preserving the existing chapter contract and ending state. Verified revised word count: 4667.

Five targeted revisions across the cited spans. Maren self-corrects at line 127 (false start on the bounty insight: 'It's not — ' before restarting), trails off at line 275 (grievance list breaks on Derry's name: 'and — ' with 'He couldn't get past Derry'), and redirects at line 383 (clean negative list collapses into concrete observation: 'Not for whatever — ' then pivots to the ash test). Aldric's formal framework cracks twice: at line 147 his contamination explanation breaks mid-sentence ('Gave the opening a —') before he restarts with an admission that his framework is insufficient, and at lines 225-229 his report-cadence collapses on the word 'buried' ('I can say I was —'), forcing a restart as raw admission ('I was buried alive'). All revisions preserve Aldric's low-contraction formality and Maren's high-contraction colloquial register. No must-land beats, tracked payoffs, or continuity facts were altered.
